chunks.text:
**CORE ENTITIES:**
- Equipment (the 50 assets) 
- EquipmentType / Category 
- Manufacturer - Project / Construction Site 
- Employee / User

**OPERATIONAL LOG ENTITIES:**
- FuelLog 
- MaintenanceSchedule 
- ServiceRecord 
- BreakdownLog 
- RepairLog (with parts + labor) 
- EquipmentTransfer (chain of custody) 
- AccidentIncidentLog

**REFERENCE/LOOKUP TABLES:**
- ref_equipment_status (Active, Under Repair, Idle, Decommissioned) 
- ref_fuel_type (Diesel, Petrol, CNG) 
- ref_transfer_status (Requested, Approved, In-Transit, Delivered)

**REFERENCE/LOOKUP TABLES:** (continued)
- ref_user_role (Site Engineer, Workshop Mechanic, Fleet Manager)
The EMS database was designed by first reading the project brief and extracting every real-world object that needed to be tracked — machines, sites, people, fuel events, breakdowns, repairs. Converted these into the entities(tables).
The core design decision was separating what assets are from what happens to them. Master tables hold the permanent records. Log tables record every event with a timestamp.
Normalization(3NF to eliminate data redundancy and prevent update anomalies) was applied throughout so no data is duplicated — manufacturer details live in one place, status values live in one place, roles live in one place.
Every design choice was made with Phase 4 in mind. Columns like engine hours, severity, and occurred at were added specifically because the ML models will need them later.

**REFERENCE/LOOKUP TABLES:** (continued)

**REFERENCE/LOOKUP TABLES:** (continued)
**27  | EMPLOYEE                      | EQUIPMENT                         ** **| 1:N  ** ** | YES (created_by)**
**** DUAL FK (same table, two roles, two columns):**
**  EQUIPMENT_TRANSFER has:**
**    from_project_id  → PROJECT  (origin)**
**    to_project_id    → PROJECT  (destination)**
**    requested_by     → EMPLOYEE (Site Engineer)**

**REFERENCE/LOOKUP TABLES:** (continued)
**    approved_by      → EMPLOYEE (Fleet Manager)**
**** THE ONLY 1:1 IN THE ENTIRE SCHEMA:**

**  BREAKDOWN_LOG → REPAIR_LOG**
**  Enforced by UNIQUE constraint on REPAIR_LOG.breakdown_id**
**  Every breakdown has exactly one repair resolution.**
**  This is how MTTR is calculated.**
**Here we have the System Architecture**
**The Role Based Access Control for different Users**
This is what transfer_status_id will cycle through:
The two step decision procedure (Approved by the Fleet Manager)*.
****  A person must be held responsible for any actions committed like assigning ****a new equipment. If not we lose the audit trail of who’s responsible if the equipment shows up damaged or missing. **
